statement.focus_question: Does the set of integers which are the sum of at most $r$ $r$-powerful numbers have density $0$?
statement.normalized: Let $r\geq 3$. A number $n$ is $r$-powerful if for every prime $p$ which divides $n$ we have $p^r\mid n$. Are there infinitely many integers which are not the sum of at most $r$ many $r$-powerful numbers? Does the set of integers which are the sum of at most $r$ $r$-powerful numbers have density $0$?
statement.original:
Let $r\geq 3$. A number $n$ is $r$-powerful if for every prime $p$ which divides $n$ we have $p^r\mid n$.

Are there infinitely many integers which are not the sum of at most $r$ many $r$-powerful numbers? Does the set of integers which are the sum of at most $r$ $r$-powerful numbers have density $0$?
